body.title: Migrate claude_budget.db SQLite → Postgres + delete file
body.result: Budget tracking migrated SQLite → Postgres (commit c246bdfa). Migration m053 creates infra.spend / infra.budget_config / infra.balance_history (BIGSERIAL ids, TIMESTAMPTZ, indexed). budget.py uses make_home_proxy + bare table names (infra in search_path). One-shot migration script moved real data: 722 spend / 2 config / 2178 balance_history rows. SQLite file + hardlink twin deleted. Two latent timestamp-subscript bugs fixed (get_balance_trajectory + _tool_spend_history). 6 regression tests pass; full TheIgors suite 4187/0 (32 skip).
body.description:
lab/utility_closet/budget.py:59 still writes SQLite to ~/.agent_datacenter/Igor-wild-0001/claude_budget.db. Unlike the other two .db files, this one likely lacks a Postgres mirror — actual migration needed (not just finish removal). Per Akien 2026-05-01: remove; migrate if necessary.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/budget.py:59 (writer), ~/.agent_datacenter/Igor-wild-0001/claude_budget.db (282KB; migrate then delete)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (CLAUDE.md)
**Scope boundary:** in — design budget Postgres schema; one-shot migration script; switch budget.py writer to Postgres; verify CC sessions still record budget; delete .db; out — utility_closet → agent_datacenter migration (separate ticket T-capability-extraction-from-igor)
**Test plan:** new test_budget_postgres covers record/read/aggregate; budget round-trip after migration matches pre-migration counts; existing CC budget reads still work
